title: Twitch VOD Track ignored when Enhanced Broadcasting is enabled
author: Steliius
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 11

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.1.2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/5JH8bG2rlrDTRKOs

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

Spotify should not be present in the Twitch VOD.

### Current Behavior

Spotify is present in the Twitch VOD.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Connect OBS to Twitch using the integrated account connection.
2. Enable Enhanced Broadcasting.
3. Configure Twitch VOD Track (Track 2).
4. Add Spotify using Application Audio Capture.
5. Exclude Spotify from Track 2 in Advanced Audio Properties.
6. Start streaming.
7. Stop the stream and inspect the Twitch VOD.

### Anything else we should know?

- Local recording of the VOD track is correct (Spotify is NOT present).
- The exact same OBS profile and audio routing work correctly when Enhanced Broadcasting is disabled.
- OBS log shows `vod_track_audio=true`.
- I tried creating a new obs profile from scratch, using the streaming key and streaming with twitch account connected, and even tried with "Ignore streaming service settings" checked and unchecked
- The issue only occurs when Enhanced Broadcasting is enabled.
- With the exact same OBS profile and audio routing, disabling Enhanced Broadcasting immediately restores the expected Twitch VOD Track behavior. (Streaming is then limited to 1080p.)

title: Linux/Wayland: OBS does not persist restore tokens from xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland
author: KeroeSparrow
body:
### Operating System Info

Other

### Other OS

CachyOS / Arch Based

### OBS Studio Version

32.1.2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/eQR1UJytroMGHOV7

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

OBS should persist a non-empty restore token in the scene collection, for example:

```json
"RestoreToken": "some-token-value"
```

On the next OBS launch, OBS should reuse the restore token and avoid prompting again when possible.


### Current Behavior

OBS stores an empty restore token:

```json
"RestoreToken": ""
```

Native OBS:

```text
~/.config/obs-studio/basic/scenes/Untitled.json
11:                "RestoreToken": ""
```

Flatpak OBS:

```text
~/.var/app/com.obsproject.Studio/config/obs-studio/basic/scenes/*.json
73:                "RestoreToken": ""
```

OBS prompts for source selection again on subsequent launches.


### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start OBS on Hyprland.
2. Add a PipeWire screen capture source.
3. In `hyprland-share-picker`, select a window or monitor.
4. Enable/check “Allow restore token”.
5. Confirm the selection.
6. Close OBS cleanly.
7. Inspect the OBS scene collection:

```bash
grep -n '"RestoreToken"' ~/.config/obs-studio/basic/scenes/*.json
```

For Flatpak OBS:

```bash
grep -n '"RestoreToken"' ~/.var/app/com.obsproject.Studio/config/obs-studio/basic/scenes/*.json
```


### Anything else we should know?

### Environment

```text
obs-studio 32.1.2-7.1
xdg-desktop-portal 1.22.1-2.1
xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland 1.3.12-2.1
hyprland 0.55.4-1.1
pipewire 1:1.6.7-1.1
wireplumber 0.5.15-1.1
```

Distribution: CachyOS / Arch-based rolling release
Session: Hyprland on Wayland
Portal backend for ScreenCast: `hyprland.portal`

### Installed portal backends

`xdg-desktop-portal -v` shows:

```text
XDP: loading /usr/share/xdg-desktop-portal/portals/gtk.portal
XDP: loading /usr/share/xdg-desktop-portal/portals/hyprland.portal
XDP: loading /usr/share/xdg-desktop-portal/portals/kwallet.portal
```

For ScreenCast specifically:

```text
XDP: Found 'hyprland' in configuration for org.freedesktop.impl.portal.ScreenCast
XDP: Using hyprland.portal for org.freedesktop.impl.portal.ScreenCast (default config)
XDP: Providing portal org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast
```
So KDE portal is not involved in ScreenCast selection.

### Notes

[xdph.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29848254/xdph.log)
The important part is that the portal backend appears to send a restore token:

```text
[screencopy] Sent restore token ...
```

and `xdg-desktop-portal` appears to receive/replace restore data with a token:

```text
XDP: Replacing restore data received from portal impl with a token
```

However, OBS still persists:

```json
"RestoreToken": ""
```

This suggests the restore token is generated by the portal path, but it does not end up saved in the OBS PipeWire source settings.

This was tested with both native OBS and Flatpak OBS, and both result in an empty `RestoreToken`.

title: OBS doesn't capture audio on Linux (empty frames from PulseAudio?)
author: iijj22gg
body:
### Operating System Info

Other

### Other OS

Arch Linux

### OBS Studio Version

Git

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

21.1.2 or later

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/PW91QkJlJnwPWRk6

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

Audio capture works. The peak meters show audio, and the audio gets encoded into the output.

### Current Behavior

Audio capture doesn't work. The pipewire graph looks correct, but the peak meters are empty and no audio from the default audio capture path gets encoded.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Add a new audio capture, or use an existing one.
2. Configure it, or double-check it is set to capture an active audio output.
3. Observe the peak meters or encode video


### Anything else we should know?

This occurs in the Flatpak build, the latest stable and beta releases as well as the latest git pull. (July 7, 2026). This only seems to affect PulseAudio (on PipeWire) and not Jack or PipeWire (screen capture).

I tried checking my PulseAudio backend by configuring applications such as Audacity to use PulseAudio, and using `parecord`. They worked normally.

I attempted to use GDB, but I'm not very experienced with it and didn't have much idea what I was looking for. I looked at `pa_stream_peek()` and found that `data` always had data, and `frames` was always empty (all zeros). I'm not sure, but I think `frames` here is supposed to be the audio data.

For now, JACK seems to work just fine for audio capture.

title: OBS Not Detecting ANY Desktop and/or Source Audio
author: MEGAF4IL-Git
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 11

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.1.2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/ei8f2NkgPZSBYZZE

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

What I expected to happen was for OBS Studio to properly detect audio so I can stream with audio.

### Current Behavior

OBS Studio is refusing to detect ANY audio other than my microphone, no matter what I do.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open OBS
2. Have audio playing in literally anything that has audio
3. See OBS isn't detecting audio

I DO NOT KNOW HOW TO REPLICATE THIS

### Anything else we should know?

It's been a couple months since I last tried to stream. Today I went to go stream, then noticed OBS was not picking up any Desktop/Game/Source audio. Yes, I've been keeping my OBS Studio up-to-date.

It detects and picks up my microphone audio, but it does not pick up any audio from anything other than my microphone.

I tried Display Capture, Game Capture, Window Capture, Audio Input Capture, and Audio Output Capture.

I've checked ALL my audio settings, verified OBS files, reinstalled OBS, made sure nothing's muted, tried all the monitoring settings, triple checked my Voicemeeter settings, changed default audio devices and everything in Windows' volume/audio mixer, changed bitrates, changed audio bitrates, checked all the monitoring settings (monitoring is not enabled for anything in my OBS Audio Mixer), updated everything (even whatever windows updates I had today), rebooted my PC 3 times, used DDU to reinstall my Adrenaline 26.6.4 drivers because DDU removes my AMD Audio Bus. 
I AM NOT REINSTALLING OR DOWNGRADING ADRENALINE DRIVERS AGAIN, SO DON'T ASK THAT.

I haven't changed anything in my OBS Studio settings, nor my Voicemeeter settings, and also not my Windows Audio settings, since the last time I successfully used OBS without any problems. However, apparently OBS Studio recently had its Audio Mixer overhauled, that might have something to do with this. 


I am using:
AMD Adrenaline 26.6.4 GPU Drivers
AMD Chipset Software: v8.05.04.516

title: macOS: Browser source (CEF) crash on CrBrowserMain — EXC_BAD_ACCESS / null write during autorelease pool drain (obs-browser 32.1.2, macOS 26.5.1, Apple Silicon)
author: adigaikwad097-gif
body:
### Summary

On macOS (Apple Silicon), OBS crashes with `EXC_BAD_ACCESS (SIGSEGV)` on the `CrBrowserMain` main thread. The crash happens inside an autorelease pool drain while an `NSArray` is being deallocated: one of the objects in the array gets a `release` message sent to it after it is already gone, so the app writes to a null pointer and dies.

I have a scene with Browser sources, and the `obs-browser` plugin (CEF) is loaded and appears at the top of both crash backtraces. I caught the crash twice within a few minutes, from **two different triggers**, but both land on the exact same code path.

This looks like a browser-source object (CEF) being released twice / released after it is already freed during Cocoa's autorelease pool cleanup.

### Steps to reproduce

I do not have a 100% reliable single click that reproduces it, but I hit it twice in ~6 minutes with these two triggers:

**Crash 1 — reordering a source by dragging**
1. Have a scene that contains one or more Browser sources.
2. In the Sources list, grab a source and drag it up/down to reorder it.
3. On drop, OBS crashes.

**Crash 2 — no drag, idle**
1. Same scene with Browser sources loaded.
2. OBS is just running (normal event loop, no drag). It crashed on its own a few minutes after relaunch.

Both crashes are on `CrBrowserMain`, both are a null write (`far: 0x0`) during an autorelease pool drain releasing an `NSArray`, with `Chromium Embedded Framework` as the top two frames and the failing Objective-C selector being `release`.

### Environment

- **OBS Studio:** 32.1.2 (24742303888), arm64 native
- **macOS:** 26.5.1 (25F80)
- **Hardware:** Apple Silicon (Mac16,10)
- **Plugin loaded:** `com.obsproject.obs-browser (32.1.2)` — `/Applications/OBS.app/Contents/PlugIns/obs-browser.plugin/Contents/MacOS/obs-browser`
- **Install:** official OBS release build (not self-compiled)

**Browser sources in the scene collection (4):**
| Name | Source |
|---|---|
| `test` | local file `overlay.html` (a static stats overlay) |
| `subscribe alerts` | remote page — Streamlabs alert box (`https://streamlabs.com/alert-box/...`) |
| `good night` | local file `goodnight.html` |
| `be right back` | local file `brb.html` |

The only remote/live page is the Streamlabs alert box; the other three are simple local HTML files.

### Crash details

Both reports:
- **Exception:** `EXC_BAD_ACCESS (SIGSEGV)`, `KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS at 0x0000000000000000`
- **Fault:** `far: 0x0  esr: 0x92000046 (Data Abort) byte write Translation fault`
- **Triggering thread:** Thread 0 `CrBrowserMain`, `com.apple.main-thread`
- **Failing selector:** `release`

**Crash 1 backtrace (drag trigger) — Thread 0:**
```
0   Chromium Embedded Framework   ... + 17000048
1   Chromium Embedded Framework   ... + 16999480
2   CoreFoundation                ___forwarding___ + 216
3   CoreFoundation                _CF_forwarding_prep_0 + 96
4   CoreFoundation                __RELEASE_OBJECTS_IN_THE_ARRAY__ + 116
5   CoreFoundation                -[__NSArrayM dealloc] + 148
6   libqcocoa.dylib               0x107954000 + 515284
7   libobjc.A.dylib               AutoreleasePoolPage::releaseUntil(objc_object**) + 204
8   libobjc.A.dylib               objc_autoreleasePoolPop + 244
9   CoreFoundation                _CFAutoreleasePoolPop + 32
10  AppKit                        NSCoreDragReceiveMessageProc + 1992
11  HIServices                    CallReceiveMessageCollectionWithMessage + 116
12  HIServices                    DoMultipartDropMessage + 96
13  HIServices                    DoDropMessage + 56
14  HIServices                    DragInApplication + 952
15  HIServices                    CoreDragStartDraggingAsync + 572
16  AppKit                        -[NSCoreDragManager _dragUntilMouseUp:initialEvent:async:] + 1204
...
21  QtGui                         QDragManager::drag(QDrag*) + 368
22  QtGui                         QDrag::exec(QFlags<Qt::DropAction>, Qt::DropAction) + 120
23  QtWidgets                     QAbstractItemView::startDrag(QFlags<Qt::DropAction>) + 576
25  QtWidgets                     QListView::mouseMoveEvent(QMouseEvent*) + 72
```

**Crash 2 backtrace (no drag, idle) — Thread 0:**
```
0   Chromium Embedded Framework   ... + 17000048
1   Chromium Embedded Framework   ... + 16999480
2   CoreFoundation                ___forwarding___ + 216
3   CoreFoundation                _CF_forwarding_prep_0 + 96
4   CoreFoundation                __RELEASE_OBJECTS_IN_THE_ARRAY__ + 116
5   CoreFoundation                -[__NSArrayM dealloc] + 148
6   libqcocoa.dylib               0x10436c000 + 515284
7   CoreFoundation                __RELEASE_OBJECTS_IN_THE_ARRAY__ + 116
8   CoreFoundation                -[__NSArrayM dealloc] + 148
9   libobjc.A.dylib               AutoreleasePoolPage::releaseUntil(objc_object**) + 204
10  libobjc.A.dylib               objc_autoreleasePoolPop + 244
11  CoreFoundation                _CFAutoreleasePoolPop + 32
12  CoreFoundation                __CFRunLoopPerCalloutARPEnd + 48
13  CoreFoundation                __CFRunLoopDoSource0 + 204
14  CoreFoundation                __CFRunLoopDoSources0 + 332
15  CoreFoundation                __CFRunLoopRun + 820
...
27  QtCore                        QCoreApplication::exec() + 216
28  OBS                           0x100b18000 + 1110500
```

### Notes

- This appears related to earlier macOS browser-source crashes on `CrBrowserMain` (e.g. #4059, #3097) and to reports of a browser-view object being disposed twice.
- I am on the latest stable OBS (32.1.2), so there is no newer version for me to try.
- macOS 26.5.1 is a very recent OS build, which may be part of the trigger.
- The two backtraces come from the same OBS session-run window (crashes at 20:16 and 20:22 local time). The second (idle) crash happened shortly after relaunching from the first.

I have the two full macOS crash reports (`.crash` / diagnostic text). Happy to attach them or provide the OBS log file if that helps narrow it down.

title: OBS does not survive DWM restart
author: felixfour
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 11

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.1.2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/JZs1OgFLe9ri2FO8

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

After restarting dwm.exe, OBS should be able to continue video capture without freezing

### Current Behavior

OBS freezes at the last frame that was on the desktop before dwm.exe was killed. Toggling on or off different graphics sources does nothing, it remains frozen

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Command Prompt as Admin
2. `taskkill /f /im dwm.exe & dwm` (note: C:\Windows\System32 SHOULD be in your path. if it isn't, use absolute address)
3. OBS graphics capture is frozen, with the only solution being restarting the program


### Anything else we should know?

Restarting DWM is sometimes required when patching it or if it has issues, similar to how one must often `taskkill /f /im explorer.exe & explorer` when that bugs out

title: Losing Additional Canvas with Enhanced Broadcasting after changing Output Resolution
author: ZipFile
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 11

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.1.2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/A1WHch3fwQgpAENB

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

Selected `Additional Canvas` must always be included in "Go live POST data".

### Current Behavior

Selected `Additional Canvas` disappears from "Go live POST data" after changing main canvas settings (e.g. output resolution) and never appears there again until restart. Which leads to wrong (automatic) configuration.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. prepare everything for stream:
a) enable `Enhanced Broadcasting`
b) select `Additional Canvas`
c) enable `Bandwidth Test Mode`
d) do other necessary preparations
e) restart OBS
2. do a test stream, check logs to confirm everything is ok
3. change `Output (Scaled) Resolution`
4. do a test stream, check logs to confirm additional canvas is not included in `Go live POST data`, neither any encoders for additional canvas being initialized
5. (optionally) revert changes from pt.3, try running stream again, behavior the same as in pt.4.

### Anything else we should know?

_No response_

title: Fails to build on Gentoo: `no template named 'function' in namespace 'std'`
author: LinuxUserGD
body:
### Operating System Info

Other

### Other OS

Gentoo Linux (amd64 musl libc, LLVM/libc++ profile)

### OBS Studio Version

Git

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

https://github.com/obsproject/obs-studio/commit/27ac23e

### OBS Studio Log URL

N/A

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

OBS Studio compiles fine adding the functional header (see https://en.cppreference.com/cpp/utility/functional/function)

```patch
--- a/frontend/utility/BasicOutputHandler.hpp
+++ b/frontend/utility/BasicOutputHandler.hpp
@@ -7,6 +7,7 @@
 #include <util/dstr.hpp>
 
 #include <future>
+#include <functional>
 
 #define RTMP_PROTOCOL "rtmp"
 #define SRT_PROTOCOL "srt"

```

### Current Behavior

- [build.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29514616/build.log)

1. add keyword for [`obs-studio-9999.ebuild`](https://codeberg.org/gentoo/gentoo/raw/branch/master/media-video/obs-studio/obs-studio-9999.ebuild)
`echo 'media-video/obs-studio **' >> /etc/portage/package.accept_keywords/obs-studio`

2. compile OBS Studio
 `emerge =media-video/obs-studio-9999`

### Anything else we should know?

_No response_

title: NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK does not load
author: Fenrirthviti
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 11

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.2.0-beta2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/S99FcVMJrvDyeezB

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK should be properly loaded and enable the NVIDIA audio effect filters.

### Current Behavior

The NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK fails to load properly.

```
11:53:16.245: [NVIDIA Audio Effects:] version: 1.6.1.2
11:53:17.557: [NVIDIA Audio Effects:] NVIDIA denoiser disabled, redistributable not found or could not be loaded.
11:53:17.581: [NVIDIA VIDEO FX]: NVIDIA VIDEO FX version: 0.7.6.0
11:53:17.843: [NVIDIA VIDEO FX]: enabled, redistributable found
```

Notably (maybe?), if you have an older version of the SDK installed, it is found and provides an error saying it is outdated:

```
11:48:22.642: [NVIDIA Audio Effects:] version: 1.3.0.22
11:48:22.642: [NVIDIA Audio Effects:]: SDK is outdated. Please update both audio & video SDK.
11:48:22.642: Required SDK versions, audio: 1.6.1; video: 0.7.6
11:48:22.670: [NVIDIA Audio Effects:] NVIDIA denoiser disabled, redistributable not found or could not be loaded.
```

After updating from the outdated SDK to 1.6.1.2, it no longer can be found by OBS.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install NVIDIA Audio Effects SDK version 1.6.1.2
2. Run OBS Studio 32.2 Beta2
3. Try to add an NVIDIA Audio Effects filter
4. Observe the filters are missing as the SDK could not be loaded


### Anything else we should know?

This may have been caused by https://github.com/obsproject/obs-studio/pull/11569

title: display capture preview and recording flickers and ghosting
author: inkgamingg-creator
body:
### Operating System Info

Other

### Other OS

Linux Fedora Bazzite Wayland KDE plasma

### OBS Studio Version

32.2.0-beta2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/logs/0LedV1B8GTo0xoG3

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

when hovering over task bar and desktop icons it shouldnt flicker and also when opening windows there shouldnt be a ghosting effect

### Current Behavior

task bar flickering occasionally and windows flicker when hovering over with mouse.there is also ghosting.

### Steps to Reproduce

open close a folder or window and close it causing ghosting .1.
hover over desktop icon or taskbar icon .2.
3.
...


### Anything else we should know?

It happens in recording and capture preview.Opera browser also might be delayed while switching tabs on obs preview/recording
im using two monitors of the same exact kind.the ghosting is of leftover windows untill mouse moves.
ghosting gets better with higher fps on obs but is too much for storage.the ghosting seems fixed or better in safe mode.safe mode also doesnt have corruption errors like normal obs has.this is a minor issue but would love to be fixed asap ive tried everything from x11 and different settings with chatgpts assistance.,OBS Studio: 32.1.2 (also tested 32.1.1 with the same behavior)
OS: Bazzite 44 (Fedora Kinoite)
Desktop: KDE Plasma 6 (Wayland)
Session: Wayland
GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070
NVIDIA Driver: 610.43.02
PipeWire: 1.6.6
Kernel: Linux 7.0.9-ogc3.2.fc44.x86_64

title: Can see OBS getting audio from the source through the Windows Mixer, but not OBS's embedded mixer; due to hidden source taking audio priority instead of the visible one
author: Zachkey
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 10

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.2.0-beta2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/tools/analyzer?log_url=https%3A%2F%2Fobsproject.com%2Flogs%2F2QCQMUSIsOOyWax7

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

I expect OBS to disable the audio being used by hidden sources until it's un-hidden (and video if that's the case).

### Current Behavior

OBS plays audio through hidden sources (like window capture), leading to it prioritizing that hidden source; instead of the visible one. 

### Steps to Reproduce

1.  Make two scenes
2. Add a windows capture source (or i think game too) to both scenes
3. Select the same game/window with audio coming out of it on both sources
4. Hide the window/game capture source on the one with audio coming out
5.  Go back to the visible one, and notice it doesn't play any audio; because it's being used by a hidden source instead of prioritizing the visible one (and removing the audio from the hidden one until it's unhidden)


### Anything else we should know?

Took me a LONG time to figure this out. At the very least, the other sources shouldn't be active when switching to another scene.

title: When clicking a source, going to another scene; then going back, if you click delete with the source highlighted, it'll delete everything other than the source you selected.
author: Zachkey
body:
### Operating System Info

Windows 10

### Other OS

_No response_

### OBS Studio Version

32.2.0-beta2

### OBS Studio Version (Other)

_No response_

### OBS Studio Log URL

https://obsproject.com/tools/analyzer?log_url=https%3A%2F%2Fobsproject.com%2Flogs%2F2QCQMUSIsOOyWax7

### OBS Studio Crash Log URL

_No response_

### Expected Behavior

When I select a source in one scene, go to another scene, then go back to the previous scene; I expect the highlighted source to get deleted,

### Current Behavior

EVERYTHING ELSE but the highlighted source gets deleted in the scene.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Click a scene
2. Click a source
3. Go to a different scene
4. Go back to the previous scene
5. Press the delete button on your keyboard, click yes, and it'll delete everything but the selected source


### Anything else we should know?

Have a nice day please!
